items.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google has been actively releasing major Gemini updates through early 2026. The most significant model announcement is Gemini 3.1 Pro, which achieves a 77.1% score on ARC-AGI-2 — more than double the reasoning performance of Gemini 3 Pro. It supports advanced agentic workflows, animated SVG generation from text prompts, and is available via the Gemini app (AI Pro/Ultra tiers), NotebookLM, Vertex AI, AI Studio, and the Gemini API. Google also released Gemini 3 Flash, a speed-optimized frontier mo

items.title: Gemini March 2026 Updates: Doubled Reasoning, Workspace Integration, and More
items.author: theagencyjournal.com
items.snippet: As of early April 2026, the Gemini API has seen a significant wave of new model releases and feature updates. The headline release is Gemini 3.1 Pro, which offers substantially improved reasoning capabilities (scoring 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2) and is now the recommended successor to the deprecated Gemini 3 Pro Preview (shut down March 9, 2026). Alongside it, Gemini 3.1 Flash Live launched as the latest audio-to-audio (A2A) model optimized for real-time, low-latency voice dialogue. New creative media m

items.title: Google AI Studio Launches Full-Stack Vibe Coding with Antigravity Agent and Firebase
items.author: blockchain.news
items.snippet: Google AI Studio has received a major update in early 2026 centered around the Antigravity agentic development platform. The headline feature is a full-stack 'vibe coding' experience that combines the Antigravity coding agent with built-in Firebase integration, allowing developers to convert natural language prompts into production-ready apps with databases, authentication, and real-world service connections. The agent proactively detects when an app needs a database or login, provisioning Cloud

items.title: Boston Dynamics' next-gen humanoid robot will have Google DeepMind DNA
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: In March and early April 2026, Google AI (@GoogleAI) and Google DeepMind (@GoogleDeepMind) made several notable announcements. On the AI safety front, Google DeepMind unveiled the first empirically validated toolkit to measure AI manipulation in real-world settings, providing standardized benchmarks for evaluating model behaviors like persuasion, deception, and coercion — a significant milestone for enterprise AI safety audits. Google also released its March 2026 AI update recap, highlighting ex

items.title: Migrate to the Google GenAI SDK | Gemini API | Google AI for Developers
items.author: ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API provides two complementary mechanisms for getting predictable, machine-readable output: Structured Outputs and Function Calling. Structured Outputs allow developers to configure Gemini models to generate responses that strictly adhere to a provided JSON Schema by setting `response_mime_type` to `application/json` and supplying a `response_schema` (compatible with Pydantic models via `model_json_schema()`). This ensures type-safe, parseable results and is ideal for extracting struc

items.title: Releases · google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization (github.com/google-gemini) remains highly active in 2026, with the flagship repository being google-gemini/gemini-cli — an open-source AI agent that brings Gemini capabilities directly into the terminal. The gemini-cli project follows a structured release cadence: weekly stable releases every Tuesday at 20:00 UTC, weekly preview releases at 23:59 UTC Tuesdays, and nightly builds published daily at midnight UTC. Recent versions include v0.35.x stable and v0.3

items.title: Get started with Genkit Monitoring
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is an open-source AI framework by Google available in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python, designed to help developers build production-grade AI-powered applications. The official documentation at firebase.google.com/docs/genkit covers the full lifecycle: from initial setup (installing packages, configuring API keys like Gemini or OpenAI) to creating typed flows, testing in the built-in Developer UI, and deploying to Cloud Functions or any Node.js platform. Core guides cover ge

items.title: Announcing Firebase Genkit for Node.js 1.0
items.author: firebase.blog
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework built and maintained by Google, available for JavaScript/TypeScript (stable), Go (beta), and Python (alpha). It provides a unified, code-centric API for text generation, structured output, streaming, tool calling, and agentic workflows. The framework centers around 'flows' — observable, streamable functions that can be deployed as API endpoints and tested via a local developer UI. Genkit reached Node.js 1.0 in February 2025 and has its own standalone site at
